notes_for_consumers: Feeds environment prompts and footage queries. Southeast winter renders WARMER, GREENER, and more church-and-football than GL/NE — Year-End reads warm-and-bright, not snow. Summer is the defining season. Bible Belt is visible in the built environment; football/faith/fishing/family are the anchors. High-confidence climate-and-landscape is operative; sensory mood is creative reference.

winter.retail_moment: Big Finish (Dec), Black Friday (Nov), Start Something New (Jan)
winter.weather: mild; coastal SE and FL stay warm (60s-70s, occasional 80s FL); inland (Atlanta, Nashville, Birmingham) freezing nights and 50s daytime; Appalachian ridge sees real cold and occasional snow but not sustained; ice storms more disruptive than snow; coastal gray-and-damp mornings, sunny afternoons typical
winter.light: soft, warmer than NE/GL at the same latitude (less cloud cover); pine canopy keeps green year-round; long shadows midday
winter.landscape: pine-dominated forest stays green; live oak with Spanish moss in coastal SE; bald cypress in swampy areas; brick ranch and mid-century neighborhoods; churches everywhere; tobacco/cotton agricultural infrastructure rural; NASCAR culture visible
winter.activity_rhythm: hunting season (deer, dove, quail) through winter; fishing nearly year-round; bowl-game and college-football-playoff culture peaks Dec-Jan; church and family-gathering rhythm strong; trucks haul deer, tow boats off-season, carry firewood and feed
winter.cgi_footage_implication: Winter cells render warmer, greener, more church-and-football than GL/NE; Year-End in SE reads warm-and-bright, not snow.

spring.retail_moment: Declaration of Deals (Mar-Apr), 4x4 Day, Truck Month
spring.weather: warm to hot quickly; azaleas and dogwoods Mar-Apr; magnolias by May; humidity ramping; tornado risk Dixie Alley Mar-May, severe thunderstorms
spring.light: bright, increasingly humid, late golden hour; saturation from dogwood, azalea, redbud, wisteria
spring.landscape: bloom layer over pine-and-deciduous backdrop; garden centers/agricultural-supply busy; construction full pace; coastal charter boats ramping
spring.activity_rhythm: outdoor peak before summer humidity peaks; SEC and ACC spring football; NASCAR Cup season opens; turkey hunting; crawfish boils Gulf Coast; Easter and Mother's Day gatherings central

summer.retail_moment: Make This The Summer Event, Summer of Jeep
summer.weather: hot and humid (defining season); daily highs 90s most of the region; FL coast and Gulf hotter/stickier; afternoon thunderstorms daily; hurricane season; heat indices >100F regular
summer.light: hard, hazy in humidity; long days, bright dawn, harsh midday, dramatic afternoon storm light, long blue hour
summer.landscape: full canopy on deciduous, pine green constant; lake culture (Lanier, Murray, Hartwell, TN reservoirs, FL chain); coastal beach communities (OBX, Hilton Head, Tybee, 30A); cookout and porch culture dominates
summer.activity_rhythm: lake and boat season at peak; day compresses around heat; cookouts and family reunions; vacation travel within region

fall.weather: heat breaks slowly; September still hot inland, hurricane-season peak; real fall mid-Oct to early Nov by latitude; Appalachian leaf color late Oct-early Nov; coastal stays warmer longer; first freeze mid-Nov NC/TN/KY
fall.light: clearer than summer; mountain leaf color (yellow, orange, red) in elevation; pine green constant elsewhere
fall.landscape: football takes over the calendar — SEC Saturdays, NFL Sundays, Friday lights, HBCU homecomings; tailgate culture peak; harvest displays in Appalachian elevations
fall.activity_rhythm: hunting opens (dove September, then deer); fall fishing; boats into storage inland; trucks haul deer, tow boats, support tailgate operations, carry hunting gear
